description: Two speakers, distinct topics. Each statement must be attributed to its speaker.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.text: I picked up a wagyu A5 ribeye on discount at H Mart yesterday.

turns.speaker: Alice
turns.text: My GPU is throttling at 84C under load. Need to repaste.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.text: I rendered it sous vide at 54C for two hours then seared in tallow.

description: Three speakers discussing related topics; risk of cross-attribution.
user_request: What did each person say about the LAN party?

turns.speaker: Adam
turns.text: Hosting LAN party Saturday. Bring your own ethernet cable.

turns.speaker: Mike
turns.text: I can bring the unmanaged 8-port switch and two cat6 runs.

turns.speaker: Sara
turns.text: I'll handle pizza. Two pepperoni and one veggie from Pequods.

turns.speaker: Adam
turns.text: Cool. Tournament bracket starts at 8pm sharp, double elim.

turns.speaker: Mike
turns.text: Are we doing CS2 or Quake Champions? My ping is awful in CS2.

description: Single speaker with no name in some lines; should still NOT be attributed to ambient/narrator.

turns.speaker: Adam
turns.text: Got Hindsight 0.6.1 deployed in docker today. Recall actually returns hits now.

turns.speaker: Adam
turns.text: Mental model refresh still empty if tags_match is restrictive though.
